title: Meridian nightly build manifest
description: The 'what is this nightly made of' record (#62). One JSON document that pins, for a single nightly (or release/adhoc) build, the exact coordinates of the three artifact streams — server images (authd/worldd), client export, and mcc content artifacts (world DB SQL + client pack) — together with the git SHA and a timestamp. This is the contract the test-realm redeploy (#94) consumes to know exactly WHICH image tag + content pack + client export make up one coherent nightly. The content-hash fields reuse the existing IF-4 <-> IF-5 BLAKE3 content-hash agreement (Tools SAD 2.6); nothing here invents a parallel hashing scheme.

schema.description: Schema id + version tag, mirroring the meridian/<name>@<n> convention used by pack-manifest (schema/pack.schema.yaml).

kind.description: What produced this manifest. 'nightly' = the scheduled main-tracking build (#62/#94); 'release' = a tagged semver release (#179); 'adhoc' = a manual workflow_dispatch run.

built_at.description: UTC timestamp the manifest was generated (RFC 3339 / ISO 8601).

git.description: The exact source revision this nightly was cut from. The nightly tracks main (D-30 10 rule 5), so sha is the tip of main at build time.

short_sha.description: Short SHA — the immutable server image tag cd.yml publishes (#175).

ref.description: Git ref the build was cut from, e.g. refs/heads/main.

server.description: Server build stream — the authd + worldd GHCR images (#175-177) the redeploy stands up. tag pins the mutable coordinate; each image also carries its immutable digest so the deploy binds to exact bits, not a movable tag.

registry.description: GHCR image path prefix, e.g. ghcr.io/kwilliams312/project_meridian.

tag.description: The image tag this nightly deploys. Conventionally the git short_sha (cd.yml publishes both <short-sha> and latest for the same digest).

images.description: One entry per daemon image (authd, worldd).

tag.description: The tag resolved for this image.

digest.description: Immutable OCI content digest (sha256:...), or null when not yet resolved (e.g. a locally-generated sample with no GHCR access — the nightly workflow always resolves it).

content.description: Content stream — the mcc pipeline outputs (emit-sql #120 -> world DB SQL + world_manifest; emit-pck #121 -> client pack). content_hash is the single BLAKE3 hash both IF-4 (world_manifest.content_hash) and IF-5 (pack.manifest.content_hash) agree on (the three-way tie, Tools SAD 2.6). world_sql.sha256 is a plain file-integrity checksum of the emitted world.sql for transport verification.

hash_algo.description: The content-hash algorithm. BLAKE3, matching the mcc pipeline (Tools SAD 2.6).

content_hash.description: The IF-4 <-> IF-5 BLAKE3 content hash (64 hex). The single source of truth for 'which content is this nightly'.

mcc_version.description: The mcc compiler version that emitted the artifacts.

world_sql.description: The IF-4 world DB SQL artifact (loads into meridian_world).

path.description: Path of the artifact relative to the content build output root.

sha256.description: SHA-256 file checksum of world.sql (transport-integrity; the semantic content identity lives in content_hash).

path.description: Path of pack.manifest.json relative to the content build output root.

content_hash.description: The pack.manifest content_hash. MUST equal content.content_hash (the IF-4 <-> IF-5 tie).

client.description: Client export stream — the Godot client export version + hash (#113). Client-export CI does not exist yet, so this coordinate is a reserved placeholder: status 'pending' with null version/hash until #113 lands, at which point the nightly resolves it to status 'resolved'. Kept in the schema now so the manifest shape is stable and the follow-up is a fill-in, not a schema change.

status.description: 'pending' = client export CI (#113) not wired yet; 'resolved' = a real export was pinned.

version.description: Client export version (e.g. a build number or semver), or null when pending.

export_hash.description: Hash of the client export bundle, or null when pending.

provenance.description: Optional CI provenance for the run that produced this manifest.
